_doc: Batch 2 — 50 distinct, nested directory tree scenarios. Each is a unique transformation.

scenarios.description: Create 1 parent with 1 child (minimal tree)
Create 3 siblings at root, all with same prefix
Create tree with 2 branches of equal depth
Create then remove empty subdirectory (rmdir path)
Create tree, rename intermediate node, verify path updated
Create 5 siblings, each with a different file
setup: for d in a b c d e; do mkdir {vfs}/$d; touch {vfs}/$d/x; done

scenarios.description: Create tree then copy entire tree to another location
Create deeply nested then list with find
setup: python3 -c 'import os; p=sys.argv[1]; os.makedirs(os.path.join(p, *["d"+str(i) for i in range(5)]), exist_ok=True)' {vfs}

scenarios.description: Create dir, place file in it, verify chain walk finds the file
Create 2 dirs, swap their names via mv
Create dir with 10 children (within readdir cap)
setup: for i in $(seq 1 10); do touch {vfs}/ten/c$i; done

scenarios.description: Create parent, remove parent, parent gone but no orphan check
Create siblings with numeric suffixes (zero-padded)
setup: for i in $(seq -w 1 12); do mkdir {vfs}/n/d$i; done

scenarios.description: Create dir, copy file from Downloads into it, MD5 verify
Create tree then run find -name to verify listing
Create 3-level nested dir, ls each level
Create a 'projects' directory structure with code-like layout
Create two siblings, rename one with new name in place
Create dir, create nested file, append to it, read back
Create chain of 4 renamed dirs
Create two-level nested tree with 4 leaves
Create dir inside a dir inside a dir, remove middle
Create a directory of 8 sibling files, verify each
setup: for i in $(seq 1 8); do echo $i > {vfs}/files/f$i; done

scenarios.description: Create tree, place XLSX at bottom, MD5
Create deep dir with 4 sub-branches at deepest level
Create dir, copy ZIP into it, unzip-like extract (just extract one file)
Create 6 dirs in a row, mv all to parent
setup: for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6; do mkdir {vfs}/d$i; done
for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6; do mv {vfs}/d$i {vfs}/p/; done

scenarios.description: Create 4 dirs, all but one empty; one has 3 files
Create dir tree, then rename root to new name
Create two sibling subtrees, each 3 levels deep
Create dir with 5 files, delete all via rm wildcard
setup: for i in $(seq 1 5); do touch {vfs}/target/t$i; done

scenarios.description: Create file in dir, rename dir, file follows
Create nested structure with alternating file/dir at each level
Create dir, place 3 different corpus files inside
Create directory inside parent that already has files
Create deep nesting with files at every level
Create dir, list it, verify . and .. entries exist (readdir entries)
Create tree, count total directories recursively
Create dir, change into it, place file with relative path
Create 5 deep paths under shared parent
setup: for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do mkdir -p {vfs}/shared/path$i/deep; touch {vfs}/shared/path$i/deep/marker; done

scenarios.description: Create dir with 4 subdirs, each with own file
setup: for q in q1 q2 q3 q4; do mkdir {vfs}/quarters/$q; touch {vfs}/quarters/$q/report; done

scenarios.description: Create tree, then mv a leaf to root
Create symlink-style by hardlink if supported (skip if not)
Create dir, copy file, remove orig, file in subdir remains
Create dir with mixed files and subdirs at depth 3
Create 8 files in nested dir, ls
setup: for i in $(seq 1 8); do touch {vfs}/a/b/file$i; done

scenarios.description: Create tree, walk with find -type f
setup: for d in 1 2 3; do mkdir {vfs}/walker/d$d; for f in 1 2; do touch {vfs}/walker/d$d/f$f; done; done

scenarios.description: Create dir, then write to a deeply nested file path
Create tree, mv entire subtree to root
Create 8-deep tree, ls full depth
setup: python3 -c 'import os; p=sys.argv[1]; os.makedirs(os.path.join(p, *["d"+str(i) for i in range(8)]), exist_ok=True)' {vfs}
